verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass TM scramblase (4 TM helices per protomer; homotrimer) — the sole integral membrane protein of the core autophagy machinery. Its canonical localization is intracellular: TGN, recycling endosomes, and small vesicular-tubular clusters (VTCs). However, multiple direct experimental methods (surface biotinylation, super-resolution TIRF microscopy, APEX2-EM, pHluorin PM-fusion reporter) demonstrate that ATG9A translocates to the plasma membrane via SNARE-dependent vesicle fusion under PM damage (digitonin, SLO, gasdermin-B), nutrient starvation, and EGF/endocytic stimulation. At the PM, the protein is physically integrated in the bilayer with luminal loops accessible on the outer leaflet. It also cycles back via endocytosis (AP2-mediated, Tyr8-phosphorylation-dependent). Baseline PM pool is minor; it enlarges substantially under stress/damage. Fits dual_localization (constitutive cycling with stress-amplified PM pool). No dominant extracellular domain; outer-leaflet exposure is via small luminal loops.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The extracellular-exposed loops are small (no large ectodomain); accessibility to binders from the outer leaflet is limited but documented for the lumenal loop region via pHluorin topology studies.
